Home › Events › AI in Healthcare Marketing Week Sponsored by BPD
Event

AI in Healthcare Marketing Week Sponsored by BPD

December 8-12
Virtual Summit

Inside the Real-World AI Playbooks of Leading Health Systems

CONTACT US

Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Strategy & Innovation, BPD Amanda Todorovich Enterprise Executive Director, Digital Marketing, Cleveland Clinic Val Lopez AVP, Marketing Operations, Baptist Health South Florida Lisa D. Mason Associate Vice President of Product Design and Insights, Providence
Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Strategy & Innovation, BPD LinkedIn
Email: swierwille@bpdhealthcare.com Stephanie Wierwille is the Executive Vice President of Engagement at BPD, where she helps healthcare brands craft and deliver compelling stories across digital, traditional, and emerging channels. With deep expertise in social-first, full-funnel marketing, she guides organizations toward marketing maturity and consumer-first engagement strategies. With a decade of experience spanning healthcare and consumer brands, Stephanie has led digital and content strategy for organizations like MD Anderson Cancer Center, Cincinnati Children’s, Johns Hopkins Medicine, Delta Air Lines, Amazon, and Walmart. From launching TGI Fridays’ first social marketing initiatives to helping Delta reshape its approach to social media across operations, marketing, and corporate communications, she has been at the forefront of digital transformation. Stephanie earned her MBA from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and is an adjunct professor at The University of Oklahoma. Passionate about innovation, she continues to push the boundaries of healthcare marketing, ensuring brands connect with audiences in meaningful and measurable ways.  
Amanda Todorovich Enterprise Executive Director, Digital Marketing, Cleveland Clinic LinkedIn
Amanda Todorovich leads digital marketing for Cleveland Clinic, one of the most trusted healthcare brands in the world. As Enterprise Executive Director, she oversees content strategy, digital experience, SEO, multimedia production, social media, and platform operations. Under her leadership, ClevelandClinic.org has grown to over 1.2 billion annual sessions—driving significant brand awareness, patient acquisition, and more than $15 million in annual advertising revenue. Amanda built and scaled a healthcare publishing model inside a health system—transforming Cleveland Clinic’s digital presence into an industry-defining engine for engagement, revenue, and trust. Her team manages thousands of content assets through an in-house editorial staff and a robust production infrastructure. Their work is not only clinically accurate and HIPAA-compliant, but also search-dominant and brand-aligned. Recognized as one of the most influential voices in healthcare digital marketing, Amanda is a respected thought leader, innovation driver, and key architect of Cleveland Clinic’s global digital growth. She holds a master’s degree in communications from The University of Akron, a bachelor’s degree in public relations from Ohio Northern University, and is a Fellow of the American College of Healthcare Executives.  
Val Lopez AVP, Marketing Operations, Baptist Health South Florida LinkedIn
 Email: ValLo@baptisthealth.net Val Lopez serves as the Assistant Vice President of Marketing Operations at Baptist Health South Florida, the region’s largest not-for-profit healthcare system. In this role, he oversees web experience, digital marketing strategy, marketing technology, paid media, marketing automation, personalization, and CRM initiatives that drive patient engagement and organizational growth. With more than 20+ years of experience leading digital marketing teams, Val has played a key role in helping healthcare organizations embrace new technologies and data-driven strategies. Prior to joining Baptist Health, he founded Martek Health, a healthcare digital marketing consultancy focused on helping health systems modernize their marketing operations. He also served as AVP of Digital Marketing at the University of Miami Health System, where he advanced digital strategies to improve patient access and brand visibility. Earlier in his career, Val held leadership roles in the travel and quick service restaurant industries with National Car Rental, Alamo Rent A Car, and Burger King Corporation, experiences that sharpened his expertise in customer experience, digital operations, and marketing innovation across large-scale organizations. Val earned his bachelor’s degree from Florida International University and his MBA from the University of Miami. He is passionate about how AI can help marketing functions reshape the consumer and patient experience in healthcare, ensuring that technology not only advances efficiency but also delivers more personalized and meaningful interactions.  
Lisa D. Mason Associate Vice President of Product Design and Insights, Providence LinkedIn
 Email: Lisa.Mason@providence.org Lisa D. Mason is the Associate Vice President of Product Design and Insights at Providence working on developing transformative platform technologies for healthcare.  Her organization has launched several health technology companies and designed the Providence Digital Health Platform which serves as the digital front door for patients. Lisa designed and launched one of the 1st LLM-based healthcare messaging chatbot assistants for enhancing care access and reducing provider burden. She has done comprehensive research on how to gain patient trust through chatbot personality & avatar design—all while ensuring patient safety. Lisa is actively working on AI-based workflow automation solutions to transform the efficiency of care delivery by leveraging AI and digital technologies to create seamless, patient-centered, and personalized healthcare experiences. Lisa has more than 25 years of experience in consumer research and product design, leading teams and companies through the complexities of understanding customers and driving product direction and vision. She started her career at Microsoft, covering emerging technologies in travel, video games, consumer and enterprise software, and mobile and consumer hardware. Lisa holds an M.S. in Physics from the University of Minnesota. Download presentation

AI and Content: Turning Generative Tools into a Scalable Strategy

CONTACT US

Emily Baker AVP, Engagement Strategy, BPD Carolina Anthony Digital Marketing Strategist & Community Advocate, AdventHealth Matt Cyr Founder & President, Loop AI Agency Allie Karas Senior Vice President of Content, MJH Life Sciences
Emily Baker AVP, Engagement Strategy, BPD
Email: ebaker@bpdhealthcare.com Emily Baker is a digital marketing leader with more than a decade of experience shaping content and social strategies that drive connection and engagement. A former healthcare business reporter, she brings an investigative approach to storytelling that delivers measurable impact for healthcare organizations, and is advancing initiatives that harness AI to unlock new engagement capabilities and elevate the impact of marketing teams.  
Carolina Anthony Digital Marketing Strategist & Community Advocate, AdventHealth LinkedIn
Email: Carolina Anthony is a passionate digital marketing strategist and community advocate leading omnichannel growth for AdventHealth. With over 18 years of experience, she cultivates digital spaces that amplify messages, build trust, and transform how communities connect with health and wellness. At AdventHealth, Carolina leads content strategy, social media, reputation management, web marketing and digital project management. She’s passionate about elevating patient experience and measurable outcomes across the digital continuum. Prior to this role, she brought her strategic vision to organizations including Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts, Hilton Grand Vacations and Golf Channel/NBC Sports, always leading with an emphasis on storytelling and brand impact. What truly distinguishes Carolina is her commitment to mentorship and inclusion: she builds cross‑functional, diverse teams and champions professional growth so women can lead with confidence and creativity. A certified yoga instructor and lifelong learner, she holds a BA in Communications & Business from Florida State University and an MA from Rollins College, and she weaves these strengths into empowering others. Carolina lives in Orlando with her husband and Mini Schnauzer, Rex. When she’s not transforming digital ecosystems, she’s traveling, exploring wellness, or getting lost in a great book.  
Matt Cyr Founder & President, Loop AI Agency LinkedIn
Email: Matt Cyr is the founder of Loop, a consultancy helping healthcare and higher-ed marketing teams integrate AI in human-centered ways. He began his career leading digital marketing at Boston Children’s Hospital and Dana-Farber, then guided healthcare clients at Primacy. Through Loop, Matt helps organizations adopt AI thoughtfully — enhancing creativity, collaboration, and trust between humans and technology.  
Allie Karas Senior Vice President of Content, MJH Life Sciences LinkedIn
Email: Allie Karas is the Senior Vice President of Content at MJH Life Sciences®️, the largest privately held medical media company in North America and the leading healthcare engagement network. She leads a 150-person team spanning more than 50 healthcare brands across oncology, specialty care, managed care, and industry sciences. A strategic architect of MJH’s content transformation, Allie is spearheading a movement to pivot away from traditional editorial to a high-impact, multimedia-first, AI-powered content engine model that is redefining how healthcare audiences learn, engage, and stay ahead of the science. Allie is passionate about the intersection of technology, storytelling, and clinical impact, and brings a fresh perspective on how AI can elevate content quality, speed, creativity, and influence across modern healthcare media. Personally, Allie is highly invested in leadership development and coaching the next generation of women leaders. She lives in Pennsylvania with her husband and four children. Download presentation

Consumer Behavior in the Age of AI Search: How to Make Your Content Seen and Trusted

CONTACT US

Lacey Reichwald Director of Marketing, Aha Media Group Robbie Schneider Enterprise Social Media Manager/ Content Marketing Leader, Franciscan Health
Lacey Reichwald Director of Marketing, Aha Media Group LinkedIn
Email: Lacey oversees Aha Media Group’s website and content, such as blogs, newsletters, and social media. She also manages events, including conferences, and speaking engagements. Additionally, Lacey is a leading voice in the healthcare marketing industry’s exploration of AI, helping teams understand and implement new tools with clarity and confidence.  
Robbie Schneider Enterprise Social Media Manager/ Content Marketing Leader, Franciscan Health LinkedIn
Email: Robbie Schneider is a digital healthcare marketer with more than two decades working in the healthcare space. At Franciscan Health, a Catholic healthcare system serving Indiana and Illinois, Robbie leads the social media and blog strategy, building early wins for the health system in the AI results space. Robbie serves as a board chair for SocialMedia.org Health, a board member of the Indiana Healthcare Marketing & Public Relations Society, and a member of the faculty at Purdue Global. Robbie speaks and writes regularly on content strategy, as well as mental wellness and burnout in digital media. She is the author of the book, Social Media, Sanity & You: A Guide to Mental Wellness for the Digital Marketer. Download presentation

Delivering on the Promise of Data-Driven Transformation

CONTACT US

Anthony Cadieux Vice President of Performance Marketing, AdventHealth Anne DiNapoli Block Managing Director, Data Solutions, BPD Brian Gresh VP Chief Marketing & Communications Officer, Community Health Network Amber Welch Vice President of Marketing and Digital Platforms, Ochsner Health
Anthony Cadieux Vice President of Performance Marketing, AdventHealth LinkedIn
Email: anthony.cadieux@adventhealth.com As Vice President of Performance Marketing at AdventHealth, Anthony leads a dynamic team dedicated to harmonizing brand strategy, digital engagement, CRM enablement and analytics to drive meaningful connections with their communities. With two decades of marketing leadership experience, he specializes in integrating performance marketing with data-driven insights to enhance patient experiences and outcomes. He is committed to continuous learning and innovation, aiming to bridge the gap between technology and compassionate care. Beyond his corporate role, he is a certified meditation teacher and executive coach, passionate about fostering mindfulness and resilience in leadership.  
Anne DiNapoli Block Managing Director, Data Solutions, BPD LinkedIn
Email: adinapoliblock@bpdhealthcare.com  Anne DiNapoli Block brings over 20 years of experience in delivering advertising and marketing strategies across a wide range of industries. She specializes in integrating data, media platforms, creative, and technology to fuel marketing strategies and daily decision-making. At BPD, Anne collaborates with the full data solutions and analytics team to provide industry-leading data products, insights, audience targeting, and enhanced analytics to empower clients, particularly in the healthcare sector. Before joining BPD in 2023, Anne was a pioneer in the retail media landscape, consulting with Fortune 100 retailers like The Home Depot and Publix Super Markets to build ad networks that merged omni-channel offerings with expansive audience data integrations. Her own health journey has inspired her to bring her passion for data-driven marketing to healthcare, where she focuses on improving patient experiences and creating tailored audience solutions for large health systems.  
Brian Gresh VP Chief Marketing & Communications Officer, Community Health Network LinkedIn
 Email: bgresh@ecommunity.com Brian Gresh currently serves as Vice President, Chief Marketing & Communications Officer at Community Health Network. Brian has more than 20 years of experience in digital and consumer healthcare strategies, branding, and communications. Prior to joining Community Health Network, he served as the president of Loyal Inc., which provides health systems tools to amplify patient feedback and guide patients through their digital journey. He came to Loyal from the Cleveland Clinic, where he was executive director of digital and content marketing. There, he led digital strategy, content marketing, social media and web development, as well as industry-leading search engine optimization and search engine marketing programs. He also led the digital team at University of Utah Healthcare as senior director of interactive marketing and web. A thought leader and frequent presenter at industry events, Brian has a bachelor’s in political science and a master’s in public administration.  
Amber Welch Vice President of Marketing and Digital Platforms, Ochsner Health LinkedIn
 Email: Amber Welch serves as Vice President of Marketing and Digital Platforms at Ochsner Health, where she leads a talented team driving demand, engagement, and brand strategy for Louisiana's largest health system and private employer. Overseeing Ochsner’s marketing strategy and technology, Amber manages the organization’s web presence, digital marketing, social media programs, online reputation management, consumer insights and analytics and client relationship management. Her strategic focus is on increasing brand awareness, affinity, and patient acquisition while fostering loyalty through high-value content and seamless digital experiences. With over 15 years of expertise in digital content, healthcare marketing, and technology, Amber thrives on leveraging data, market research, and cutting-edge tools to empower online health seekers to manage their care. She’s embraced AI for marketing, ensuring a consistent brand voice across all channels while enhancing program efficiencies and output across the department. From optimizing user experiences across Ochsner’s websites to executing digital campaigns that integrate with the EMR, her mission is to connect people to Ochsner while providing seamless access to healthcare for the Gulf South’s communities. Prior to joining Ochsner in 2011, Amber worked in journalism and publications with a focus on online content and websites. She received her Master of Science in Journalism from Columbia University and a Bachelor of Arts degree in both English and Spanish from the University of California at Santa Barbara. Download presentation

AI and Data-Driven Transformation in Practice: Stories and Advice from Three Major Health Systems

CONTACT US

John Davey VP of Performance Marketing, AdventHealth Tara Nooteboom Director of Consumer Digital Strategy, UCI Health Josefer Montes Executive Director, Marketing & Branding, AdventHealth Micha Siegel Director, Product Innovation and Strategy, BPD
John Davey VP of Performance Marketing, AdventHealth LinkedIn Email: john.davey@mountsinai.org
John Davey is Vice President, Marketing Technology for the Mount Sinai Health System. John has been at Mount Sinai for three years, before which he was in similar roles at NYU and Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center. John is a seasoned marketing professional with more than 15 years of experience successfully implementing digital marketing strategies designed to meet strategic business goals for world-class health systems, academic medical centers, and research institutes.  
Tara Nooteboom Director of Consumer Digital Strategy, UCI Health LinkedIn
Email: tnootebo@hs.uci.edu Tara J Nooteboom, MS, is Director of Consumer Digital Strategy at UCI Health, an academic health system in Orange County, CA. At UCI Health, Nooteboom oversees the digital consumer journey and experience roadmap. She and her team are responsible for driving access to care, transforming digital experiences, and orchestrating brand experience. Since joining UCI Health in 2022, Nooteboom has been an instrumental player in the organization’s growth and digital transformation. Her leadership enabled the launch of the UCI Health digital front door in February 2023, followed by same-day care digital service navigation and new patient digital appointment access. These included launching the first-ever consumer website with scheduling options for the organization, since expanding to include several specialties and services and over 2000 appointments, as well as expansion of authenticated scheduling options in MyChart. Prior to joining UCI Health, Nooteboom led digital patient engagement at Rush University System for Health in Chicago. She lives in Los Angeles with her two cats, Ingrid Bergman and Humphrey Bogart.  
Josefer Montes Executive Director, Marketing & Branding, AdventHealth LinkedIn
Email: Josefer Montes, PhD, is Executive Director, Marketing & Communications at AdventHealth Georgia. He blends sales expertise, brand strategy, and data-driven storytelling to build trust, support clinicians, and expand access to care. A former professor and academic dean, he now focuses on practice—leading integrated campaigns that pair clinical accuracy with clear, human language. His work spans brand development, demand generation, stakeholder communications and more. Guided by a consumer-first mindset and AdventHealth’s promise to help people feel whole—body, mind, and spirit—he helps deliver measurable, mission-aligned results.  
Micha Siegel Director, Product Innovation and Strategy, BPD LinkedIn
Email: msiegel@bpdhealthcare.com  Micha Siegel is the Director of Product Innovation and Strategy at BPD. He’s a seasoned healthcare marketing professional who began in-house at a leading healthcare system, where he advanced marketing capabilities and introduced technologies to enhance patient engagement and streamline marketing operations. For the past three years, Micha has focused on 1:1 marketing, using data, analytics, and claims insights to create personalized, HIPAA-compliant campaigns. His passion lies in connecting data with human insights to deliver tailored messaging that drives patient engagement and measurable results. Originally from Chicago, and a self-professed lover of deep-dish pizza, he would rather be working out than sitting on his couch and has put this energy into becoming an avid triathlete. As such, he enjoys, in equal measure, running, biking and swimming, and he loves having his daughters join him in these activities. Download presentation

AI Agents and Workflow Optimization: From Idea to Impact

CONTACT US

Drew Marlatt VP, Data Products & Technology, BPD Carrie Liken Strategic Account Executive, Snowflake Victor Reiss Chief Consumer Officer, UNC Health
Drew Marlatt VP, Data Products & Technology, BPD LinkedIn
Email: amarlatt@bpdhealthcare.com Matt Cyr is the founder of Loop, a consultancy helping healthcare and higher-ed marketing teams integrate AI in human-centered ways. He began his career leading digital marketing at Boston Children’s Hospital and Dana-Farber, then guided healthcare clients at Primacy. Through Loop, Matt helps organizations adopt AI thoughtfully — enhancing creativity, collaboration, and trust between humans and technology.  
Carrie Liken Strategic Account Executive, Snowflake LinkedIn
Email: carrie.liken@gmail.com Carrie Liken currently works on the Healthcare and Life Sciences team at Snowflake. She has spent almost 20 years working at the intersection of healthcare and technology, and in that time, she has been educating organizations about the digital elements of the patient journey and how patients seek healthcare information using digital tools. Liken most recently served as the Head of Industry for Healthcare at Yext, where she led the global healthcare business, including product, strategy, partnership development, and go-to-market. During her time at Yext, she visited over 400 health systems, payer, and pharma organizations, gaining a deep understanding of the key trends and challenges within the healthcare industry. Prior to Yext, Liken spent 8.5 years at Google, where she built their healthcare provider team within Google Ads. In this role, she helped healthcare organizations understand the patient journey, the impact of digital on patient and customer acquisition, and how to drive increased revenue and sales through digital channels. Liken has been quoted in media outlets such as Forbes and Business Insider, featured on industry podcasts and frequently speaks across the country on topics related to healthcare and technology. She also maintains a Substack where she writes about the intersection of healthcare, technology, and AI. Liken holds a degree in public policy and political science from Duke University and a Master of Public Policy from the Harvard Kennedy School of Government, where she focused on health policy.  
Victor Reiss Chief Consumer Officer, UNC Health LinkedIn
 Email: Victor.Reiss@unchealth.unc.edu As Chief Consumer Officer at UNC Health, Victor Reiss leads the system strategy for consumer insights, growth marketing, brand, community engagement, and communications. His work is guided by a core belief: the future of healthcare will be defined by how we honor people’s time. Every moment returned to patients, families, and communities is an opportunity for healing, connection, and living more fully. Victor brings a thought-leader’s lens to the evolving role of consumers in healthcare—asking not only how people access care, but why their experiences succeed or fall short. He envisions a health system where friction is designed out, where consumer insights guide every decision, and where trust is earned through seamless, intuitive interactions. Prior to this role, Victor served as System Vice President of Growth Marketing and Consumer Insights at UNC Health. He also held leadership roles at Cancer Treatment Centers of America and spent more than a decade at FedEx, experiences that shaped his ability to blend human-centered insights with global-scale strategy. Victor is a Fellow of the American College of Healthcare Executives and serves on the board of the UNC Health Physicians Network. He earned his MBA from Indiana Wesleyan University and his undergraduate degree from Butler University. Download presentation

Are Hyper-Personalized Patient Journeys Our New Future? How Industry Leaders Are Using AI Agents to Get There

CONTACT US

Andrew Chang Chief Marketing Officer, UChicago Medicine Chris Hemphill Founder and Head of Applied Data Science & AI, Modular Feedback Paul Lambson Assistant Vice President Consumer Technology, Northwell Health Drew Marlatt VP, Data Products and Technology, BPD
Andrew Chang Chief Marketing Officer, UChicago Medicine LinkedIn
Email: andrew.chang@uchicagomedicine.org Andrew Chang is Chief Marketing Officer (CMO) at UChicago Medicine, where his team is using AI agents to modernize patient engagement with seamless touchpoints. Chang’s vision of agentic AI in healthcare includes automated appointment scheduling, proactive patient reminders, operational efficiency and personalization. He emphasizes reskilling teams to ensure technology improves both the patient experience and business growth while maintaining human connection at the core of healthcare.  
Chris Hemphill Founder and Head of Applied Data Science & AI, Modular Feedback LinkedIn
Email: chris@modularfeedback.com Chris Hemphill is head of Modular Feedback where he helps healthcare leaders and innovators “Reclaim the Algorithm.” Where Big Tech’s AI prioritizes clicks and doom scrolling over wellness, Healthcare AI has more serious obligations to patients, providers, and payers. Hemphill draws on experience leading & creating Healthcare AI efforts since 2018 at companies like Actium Health, VP of Applied AI and Woebot Health, Sr. Director of Commercial Intelligence. Chris’s leadership in mitigating AI’s biases & expanding outreach to underserved Black and Asian communities led to a fellowship with NYU McSilver Institute for Poverty Policy Research in 2022. Chris continues to drive health equity AI initiatives there. Outside his work in data science, Chris co-hosts a podcast, Hope, Power, and Healthcare with Melissa Reilly, who is a veteran in healthcare strategy & innovation. The mission with nearly a decade in AI and sharing the podcast: Remind healthcare leaders that they have agency and accountability in building the future of healthcare.  
Paul Lambson Assistant Vice President Consumer Technology, Northwell Health LinkedIn
 Email: Paul Lambson is AVP of Consumer Technology at Northwell Health, where he leads enterprise systems that apply AI and data to optimize customer engagement. With deep experience in marketing technology, CRM and consumer applications, Paul focuses on turning complex data into actionable insights that drive measurable outcomes in highly regulated industries.  
Drew Marlatt VP, Data Products and Technology, BPD
Email: Drew Marlatt is vice president, Data Products and Technology, at BPD, and has a career built on delivering custom, data-driven solutions across industries, from healthcare and life sciences to financial services and defense. He thrives on solving complex problems with analytics, statistics, machine learning, and generative AI—always focused on creating the simplest, most effective solutions to generate insight and improve efficiency. As an industry thought leader on data privacy and responsible AI, Drew believes cutting-edge technology must be applied with care to build trust and ensure fairness for both users and society. His approach blends innovation with responsibility, ensuring BPD’s data products deliver measurable value while upholding the highest ethical standards. Download presentation

Enterprise Design for AI

CONTACT US

Victoria Davis Group Account Director, BPD Christine Kotler Chief Marketing and Communications Officer, Baptist Health South Florida Pam Landis VP, Digital Engagement, Hackensack Meridian
Victoria Davis Group Account Director, BPD LinkedIn
Email: vdavis@bpdhealthcare.com Victoria is a passionate integrated marketer and account leader with more than 15 years of experience serving as a strategic partner, brand advocate and senior advisor for executives, encouraging them to find their brand vision. She received her Bachelor’s in Journalism from the University of Oklahoma and completed her MBA at the University of Texas – Arlington.  
Christine Kotler Chief Marketing and Communications Officer, Baptist Health South Florida LinkedIn
Email: Ckotler@baptisthealth.net Christine oversees all branding, marketing, communications, and media efforts for Baptist Health South Florida. Baptist Health is the largest not-for-profit healthcare organization in the region with 12 hospitals, 200+ outpatient locations, 28,000+ employees, more than 4,500 physicians, and 3.3 million patient encounters. Throughout her career, Christine has been committed to the not-for-profit sector, driving results, increasing customer engagement, and strengthening brand awareness, loyalty, and affinity. She was named to Becker’s 2023 "Chief Marketing Officers to Know" list and honored as a Ragan’s Top Woman in Communications – Crisis Navigator for her team’s exceptional response during the COVID-19 pandemic. Passionate about purposeful work, she also teaches healthcare marketing at the University of Miami.  
Pam Landis VP, Digital Engagement, Hackensack Meridian LinkedIn
 Email: pamela.landis@hmhn.org Pamela DeSalvo Landis is senior vice president of digital engagement at Hackensack Meridian Health, a $9 billion integrated health network in New Jersey. She leads a team responsible for making access to and management of healthcare easier for patients, consumers, providers and team members. Her team is responsible for a centralized, multi-channel Patient Access Center that supports all HMH practices and hospitals, all web sites (both internal and external), all mobile applications, virtual health tools (i.e. video visits), customer relationship management marketing, consumer analytics and insights, search engine optimization and marketing, digital marketing and patient engagement tools like MyChart, patient texting and online appointment scheduling. Prior to joining Hackensack Meridian, she led digital efforts at Atrium Health in Charlotte, N.C. and Henry Ford Health System in Detroit, MI. She began her career in healthcare at Johns Hopkins Medical Institutions and has worked in Washington, D.C. as a communications professional for lobbying organizations and a reporter for Gannett. She is a graduate of Ohio University in Athens, OH and earned a master’s degree in health informatics from the University of Illinois-Chicago. Download presentation

Closing Keynote: The Einstein Divide

CONTACT US

Jason Lesandrini, PhD Assistant Vice President of Ethics, Wellstar Health System Sameer Sethi Chief Analytics Officer, Hackensack Meridian Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Engagement, BPD
Jason Lesandrini, PhD Assistant Vice President of Ethics, Wellstar Health System LinkedIn
Email: jason.lesandrini@wellstar.org Jason Lesandrini, PhD., is an expert in ethics and leveraging innovative techniques to build ethical capacity, leaders, team, and culture. He leads the departments of ethics, advance care planning, spiritual health, and language access services for Wellstar Health System. In this role, Dr. Lesandrini provides leadership and resources to promote ethical behavior, decision-making, and conduct aligned with the system's mission, vision, and values. As an educator, Jason serves as a faculty member for the Physician Assistant Programs at Mercer University and South College and teaches undergraduates at Georgia Tech. He has also worked as an ethics resource for numerous national professional organizations including The American College of Healthcare Executives, The National Hospice and Palliative Care Organization, The American Board of Medical Specialties, and others. Jason's academic degrees include a PhD in Healthcare Ethics from Duquesne University, a Master of Arts in Philosophy from Georgia State University, and a Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy from Michigan State University. In addition, he is a Certified Healthcare Ethics Consultant (HEC-C), a Leadership Professional in Ethics and Compliance (LPEC), and Fellow of the American College of Healthcare Executives (FACHE). His research interests include the ethical implications of emerging technologies in healthcare. He has published work examining the ethics of artificial intelligence and machine learning, particularly as these technologies are increasingly integrated into clinical practice. He is widely published and has presented on ethics across the globe.  
Sameer Sethi Chief Analytics Officer, Hackensack Meridian LinkedIn
Email: sameer.sethi@hmhn.org Sameer Sethi is a seasoned leader and expert in healthcare data and analytics with a proven track record of enabling use of data and analytical techniques to drive distinctiveness and deliver transformative impact. He has focused his career on data, technology, and innovation for healthcare providers. Starting his healthcare career in EMR implementations motivated Sameer to find ways to use digitized medical data to improve patient care, influence clinical workflow and provider network operations. He has since operated at the cross-section of healthcare and technology to improve quality, access and cost-of-care delivery.  Sameer previously worked at Mount Sinai Health System, McKinsey, Bon Secours Mercy Health. His experiences in data and analytics roles across consulting and health systems gives him diverse perspectives on the challenges facing providers in data and insights enablement and technology adoption. Sameer and his team are currently focused on accelerating the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) to deliver high quality, affordable, more accessible, and more efficient healthcare. Recently, Sameer was named by Becker’s Hospital Review among leading hospital and health system chief data and analytics officers making an impact.
Stephanie Wierwille EVP, Engagement, BPD LinkedIn
 Email: swierwille@bpdhealthcare.com Stephanie Wierwille is the Executive Vice President of Engagement at BPD, where she helps healthcare brands craft and deliver compelling stories across digital, traditional, and emerging channels. With deep expertise in social-first, full-funnel marketing, she guides organizations toward marketing maturity and consumer-first engagement strategies. With a decade of experience spanning healthcare and consumer brands, Stephanie has led digital and content strategy for organizations like MD Anderson Cancer Center, Cincinnati Children’s, Johns Hopkins Medicine, Delta Air Lines, Amazon, and Walmart. From launching TGI Fridays’ first social marketing initiatives to helping Delta reshape its approach to social media across operations, marketing, and corporate communications, she has been at the forefront of digital transformation. Stephanie earned her MBA from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign and is an adjunct professor at The University of Oklahoma. Passionate about innovation, she continues to push the boundaries of healthcare marketing, ensuring brands connect with audiences in meaningful and measurable ways. Download presentation
